preamble: The shift from the alveolar nasal /n/ to the voiceless postalveolar fricative /ʃ/ is a documented palatalization process, notably attested in Eastern Ojibwe. This change is considered 'unmotivated' or 'unnatural' in some linguistic theories due to its departure from typical phonetic motivations.
phoneticEffects: Palatalization, denasalization, devoicing, change in manner (nasal to fricative), change in place (alveolar to postalveolar)

examples.note: Palatalization of /n/ to [ʃ] occurs before front vowels.

sources: Buckley, E. (2000). The Phonology of Eastern Ojibwe. University of Chicago Press.
Beguš, G. (2018). Post-nasal devoicing and the blurring process. Journal of Linguistics, 54(3), 519-557.
Beguš, G. (2019). Estimating historical probabilities of natural and unnatural processes. Phonology, 36(1), 51-97.
